title: Scalar Lax-Friedrichs weights are nonnegative under the CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 12.5, p. 232.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If -1 <= cfl <= 1, then the Lax-Friedrichs weights (1 + cfl) / 2 and (1 - cfl) / 2 are both nonnegative.
informal.expected_conclusion: Both scalar Lax-Friedrichs weights are nonnegative.

declarations.name: laxFriedrichsWeights_nonneg
declarations.statement: theorem laxFriedrichsWeights_nonneg (cfl : ℝ) (hcflLower : -1 ≤ cfl) (hcflUpper : cfl ≤ 1) : 0 ≤ laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl ∧ 0 ≤ laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl

tasks.input: Translate the CFL nonnegative-weight statement for Lax-Friedrichs into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem laxFriedrichsWeights_nonneg (cfl : ℝ) (hcflLower : -1 ≤ cfl) (hcflUpper : cfl ≤ 1) : 0 ≤ laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl ∧ 0 ≤ laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl

tasks.input: Given the weight definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using unfold, constructor, and linarith.
